seo.title: Best Falls Prevention Technology 2026 | Able Care
seo.description: The 2026 buyers guide to falls prevention software. Features, evidence, pricing and fit by segment. An honest comparison of Able Assess, SafelyYou, Nymbl and paper-based methods.

blocks.headline: Best Falls Prevention Technology 2026
blocks.subtitle: Features, evidence, pricing and fit by segment. An honest comparison.

blocks.heading: Able Assess vs traditional falls prevention methods
blocks.tldr: Fall detection tools like SafelyYou respond after a fall happens. Balance training apps like Nymbl push exercises but do not screen risk. Paper assessments take 20+ minutes, are scored subjectively and rarely feed into a care plan. Able Assess screens every resident objectively in under five minutes, stratifies risk automatically and hands the operator a standardised workflow they can roll out to every site.

values: FDA listed, CE marked, ISO 27001, NICE 2025

pulledStat.text: Objective, multifactorial falls risk screening has been shown to reduce falls by 25%.
pulledStat.source: Able Care 2026 Falls Prevention Commercialisation Strategy

studies.title: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure
Multifactorial falls risk screening outperforms single-test paper approaches
NICE 2025 falls prevention guidelines recommend objective, standardised screening

cards.title: Multi-branch Home Care Operator
cards.outcome: Caught decline six weeks earlier than their previous process. Reduced hospitalisations across 12 branches.

cards.title: US CCRC Community
cards.outcome: Reduced resident falls by 25% in the first year. Protected occupancy and reduced liability exposure.

cards.title: UK Clinical Research Team
cards.outcome: Standardised grip strength capture across three trial sites. Improved endpoint signal for GLP-1 muscle loss monitoring.
